pullRequest.title: Rename variety sprites to correct Pokémon IDs
pullRequest.body:
This adds a script that renames variety PNG files (e.g. 877-hangry.png) to their correct Pokémon IDs by querying the Pokémon species endpoint and matching varieties by name.

Resolves #174.

First run against the official-artwork folder looks correct; I'll follow up with 2–3 other folders in this PRs and keep this minimal.

pullRequest.title: feat: Add the new showdown sprites
pullRequest.body: Added the showdown sprites which have been updated [here](https://play.pokemonshowdown.com/sprites/) also added a script which allows automated adding of sprites and dry runs to check missing ones from the repo, should also resolve #184

pullRequest.title: Add icons for types per game (#191)
pullRequest.body: Closes #191 As discussed in #191: I moved already existing icons from `/sprites/types/{generation}/{game}/*.png` to `[...]/{generation}/{game}/badges/*.png` and added `[...]/icons/*.png` for each game that has these icons. In this case, icons are purely the icons, and badges are icons with the type's name next to them. (Sometimes I just can't explain stuff properly so see the example below) For games that do not have icons, I moved all images to `[...]/{generation}/{game}/badges/`. |Example Badge|Example Icon| |---|---| | ||

pullRequest.title: fix: Rename bdsp folder
pullRequest.body:
Rename `brilliant-diamond-and-shining-pearl` folder to `brilliant-diamond-shining-pearl` to match api spec

resolves: #204

pullRequest.title: Added Paldea Victory Road Badges
pullRequest.body:
resolves #208 

Added the Victory Road Badges on the same format as the rest (pngs with transparent backgrounds)

Turns out the most official source that has the images is the own game, so I printed the Badges on the best resolution possible and used python (rembg) to remove the background.

Hope the quality is good enough
